meta.lastUpdated: After Round 2 · R3 In Progress · U.S. Open · 2026-06-20

prizes.label: Overall Pool Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings, all four majors

prizes.label: Group Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings within your group of 8

participants.name: Arif Nuraney
participants.blurbShort: Rory won Augusta, Aaron Rai won Aronimink — still in first by a country mile.
participants.blurb: The two-major winner. Rory carried Augusta, Aaron Rai committed grand larceny at Aronimink, and Arif just sat back and collected cheques. Min Woo Lee and Harris English missed the cut at Shinnecock but it hardly matters. Gotterup is T60 and Aaron Rai isn't in the field, but the lead is so large it would take a meteorite to dislodge him. Annoyingly, actual skill appears to be involved.

participants.name: Matt Cavelti
participants.blurbShort: Aaron Rai's PGA miracle + Scheffler's quiet competence = a very comfortable second place.
participants.blurb: The most dangerous man not named Arif. Scheffler is T11 at Shinnecock, Tyrrell Hatton bounced back nicely, and Aaron Rai's PGA win is the gift that keeps on giving. Harris English and Si Woo Kim both missed the cut, and Aaron Rai isn't in the field, so the US Open upside is modest. But the gap to third is growing. Cavelti is playing a patient, boring long game — and it's working.

participants.name: Kevin Young
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T7, Kitayama T34 — the PGA drought is slowly being forgiven.
participants.blurb: Banked a monster Masters on Rory's back, then watched the PGA happen to other people. Shinnecock is more charitable: Sam Burns is T7, Kurt Kitayama T34, Rory T11. Brooks Koepka and Min Woo Lee both missed the cut, which was as predictable as it was painful. Russell Henley scraped in at T46. The gap to second is real but the lead is softening — Kevin needs a Burns weekend.

participants.name: Ali Pejman
participants.blurbShort: Wyndham Clark leads at -7. Ali drafted him and then forgot about him for two majors.
participants.blurb: Front-loaded everything at Augusta and has been coasting on fumes since. Then Wyndham Clark — who missed the PGA cut and contributed exactly nothing for two months — decided to lead the US Open at -7. Rory is T11, Tyrrell Hatton T34, Corey Conners T22. Brooks Koepka and Cam Smith missed the cut. If Clark wins on Sunday, Ali vaults up the standings in the most chaotic way possible. He's earned none of this. He'll take all of it.

participants.name: Adam Hill
participants.blurbShort: Rory T11 is doing the work. Jason Day WD, three others gone — pray for the weekend.
participants.blurb: Augusta hero, Aronimink ghost, Shinnecock purgatory. Jason Day withdrew, Adam Scott and Daniel Berger both missed the cut. Hideki Matsuyama T60, Russell Henley T46, Rory T11. The top two are pulling dead weight across a fairway. Without a Rory charge this weekend, Adam is watching the pack close behind him.

participants.name: Michael O'Callaghan
participants.blurbShort: Rory T11, Tyrrell Hatton T34 — but Viktor, Si Woo, Nick Taylor all gone.
participants.blurb: Rode Rory and a tidy Masters into the lead pack, then lost Viktor Hovland, Si Woo Kim and Nick Taylor to the cut. Tyrrell Hatton at least showed some backbone with a R2 68 and sits T34. Hideki Matsuyama is T60. Rory is T11 and doing everything he's being paid to do. The issue is that three of six are already packing their bags while everyone else has more horses in the race.

participants.name: Jeff Williams
participants.blurbShort: Rory, Conners, Bradley all alive — Max Homa remains unavailable for comment.
participants.blurb: Seventh overall and making a quiet case for top five. Rory T11, Corey Conners T22, Keegan Bradley T22. Patrick Cantlay and Viktor Hovland missed the cut. Max Homa was never in the field, which continues to be a wound that does not heal. Three live players with top-25 positions is actually a decent hand to play this weekend.

participants.name: Jamie MacDougall
participants.blurbShort: Rory T11, Brian Harman T11 — JJ Spaun and Adam Scott have left the building.
participants.blurb: Top eight overall, almost entirely because Rory carried a team that includes JJ Spaun — who missed the cut — and Adam Scott, same. Brian Harman is T11 and quietly making a case for himself. Hideki Matsuyama is T60. Marco Penge was never in the field. Jamie has two real horses at Shinnecock and four passengers. Bold roster construction, as always.

participants.name: Chris Cooper
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T11, Theegala T7 — Jon Rahm's 78 was a knife in the back.
participants.blurb: You have Scheffler AND Rahm and somehow only ninth — but the US Open might fix that. Sahith Theegala is your surprise star at T7, Scheffler is T11, Sungjae Im T34. Jon Rahm shot 78 in R2 and missed the cut in the most Rahm-at-a-major way possible. Brooks Koepka and Min Woo Lee also gone. This is now a two-man show — but what a two-man show.

participants.name: Phil Kuzyk
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T7, Scheffler T11 — Brooks Koepka missed the cut at +10, which is just rude.
participants.blurb: Scheffler keeps you respectable, Sam Burns is T7 and cooking. Robert MacIntyre T60 is at least in the field. Brooks Koepka shot 77 in R2 and limped off at +10 — the least he could have done. Max Homa still not in the field. Hideki Matsuyama is T60. Two genuine horses, two in the weekend as bystanders, two gone. A workable situation.

participants.name: Tom Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: JT T7, Theegala T7, Scheffler T11 — Viktor Hovland watched from the car park.
participants.blurb: Solid, balanced, no longer forgettable. JT shot 68, Sahith Theegala shot 67 — suddenly you have two players in the top seven. Scheffler adds a third at T11. Jordan Spieth is T46, Kurt Kitayama T34. Viktor Hovland missed the cut and contributed the vibe of a man who forgot his clubs. Tom Edstrand is quietly having a very good US Open.

participants.name: Rob Pettit
participants.blurbShort: JT T7, Justin Rose T22, Tyrrell Hatton T34 — Adam Scott and Nick Taylor left early.
participants.blurb: No superstar, just six guys quietly cashing cheques — and then some of them stopped. JT is your best at T7, Justin Rose at T22, Tyrrell Hatton T34. Patrick Reed and Nick Taylor missed the cut. Adam Scott missed the cut. Three solid players is enough to do some damage this weekend. Rob Pettit remains respectably, stubbornly alive.

participants.name: Jimmy Richmond
participants.blurbShort: Jon Rahm shot 78 and broke your heart. Ludvig Aberg T22 is now your lifeline.
participants.blurb: Your PGA was electric on Rahm's back. Then Shinnecock happened. Rahm shot 78 in R2 and missed the cut at +6 — a genuine disaster. Ludvig Aberg is T22, Matsuyama T60, Spieth T46, Bridgeman T60. Jake Knapp missed the cut. The Spaniard who saved the PGA has exited the building. Aberg needs to be the hero now.

participants.name: Sean Monahan
participants.blurbShort: JT T7, Scheffler T11 — Jason Day WD, Jake Knapp went home early.
participants.blurb: Scheffler and JT do the heavy lifting while the rest of the card files for emotional support. Jason Day withdrew. Jake Knapp missed the cut. Robert MacIntyre T60 is at least still breathing. Ryan Fox T46. The top two are genuinely good — the question is whether they can carry enough to move Sean up the overall standings.

participants.name: Chris Kleaman
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T22, Cameron Young T34, Ben Griffin T34 — three Masters top-threes, still sliding.
participants.blurb: Three Masters top-threes and you peaked in April. Shinnecock is more charitable: Justin Rose T22, Cameron Young T34, Ben Griffin T34, Russell Henley T46. Harris English missed the cut. Jacob Bridgeman T60. Four players in the weekend field is a solid foundation. The question is whether any of them can climb into the top ten on the weekend.

participants.name: Phil Kinninmont
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T2, Sam Burns T7, Gary Woodland T11 — Rahm's 78 was everyone's nightmare.
participants.blurb: Dead weight at Augusta, then Rahm dragged you up the PGA board. Now Shinnecock is being genuinely kind. Matt Fitzpatrick is co-leading at -3, Sam Burns is T7, Gary Woodland T11, Ben Griffin T34. Jon Rahm missed the cut with a 78 — the gift of the PGA has expired. But Phil has three players in the top 11 right now, which is more than most.

participants.name: Mike Richmond
participants.blurbShort: Xander T2, McNealy T11, Aberg T22 — three legitimate horses going into the weekend.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele shot 66 in R2 and is co-leading at -3. Maverick McNealy is T11. Ludvig Aberg T22. Russell Henley T46. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren both missed the cut. Three players in the top 25 heading into the weekend is the best this card has looked all season. Mike Richmond is suddenly very relevant.

participants.name: Gareth McDonnell
participants.blurbShort: Xander T2, Justin Rose T22, Tyrrell Hatton T34 — Shane Lowry took his ball and went home.
participants.blurb: Xander, Rose, Hatton all alive and in the top 35. Shane Lowry missed the cut at +6. Marco Penge and Aldrich Potgieter not in the field. Three quality players is a genuine weekend opportunity. If Xander wins, Gareth makes a significant move up the overall standings. The logic is clean. The execution is Xander's problem now.

participants.name: Jon Greyell
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T11, Keegan Bradley T22 — everyone else missed the cut or was never in the field.
participants.blurb: Scheffler's Masters is basically your entire season, and at Shinnecock he's T11 and still in it. Keegan Bradley T22 is a pleasant surprise. Patrick Cantlay, Si Woo Kim, Viktor Hovland and Nick Taylor all missed the cut. Max Homa not in the field. Two horses, three weeks of damage, and a prayer. Jon Greyell is playing a very short deck.

participants.name: Jay Barre
participants.blurbShort: McNealy T11, Justin Rose T22, Cameron Young T34 — Ryan Gerard missed the cut, per tradition.
participants.blurb: Justin Rose T22, Maverick McNealy T11, Cameron Young T34. Chris Gotterup T60, Jacob Bridgeman T60. Ryan Gerard missed the cut, which is the most Ryan Gerard outcome available. Three players with genuine upside heading into R3. Jay Barre is mid-pack and making a quiet case for top 20 if the weekend goes right.

participants.name: Sean Fairburn
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T2, Sam Burns T7, Ben Griffin T34 — Patrick Reed missed the cut by one.
participants.blurb: Three of six inside the top 35. Matt Fitzpatrick T2, Sam Burns T7, Ben Griffin T34, Russell Henley T46. Patrick Reed and Nicolai Hojgaard both missed the cut. If Fitzpatrick and Burns both charge on the weekend, Sean makes a legitimate run up the standings. This is the best the card has looked since the Masters.

participants.name: Rod Armstrong
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T7, Aberg T22, Conners T22 — Rahm's 78 is the straw that broke the Spaniard.
participants.blurb: Rahm's 78 in R2 was the gut punch. But Sam Burns T7, Ludvig Aberg T22, Corey Conners T22, Robert MacIntyre T60, Ryan Fox T46 — four live players is a real hand. Jon Rahm missed the cut. The Swede and the Canadian are picking up the slack the Spaniard dropped on the 18th green.

participants.name: Nils Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T11, Harman T11, Bradley T22 — Bryson DeChambeau continues to haunt you.
participants.blurb: Scheffler T11, Brian Harman T11, Keegan Bradley T22 — three decent weekend horses. Bryson shot 75 in R2 and missed the cut at +5. Harris English also gone. Alex Noren gone. The hex on Bryson is multi-major at this point. At least Scheffler and Harman showed up. Nils is mid-pack but with genuine upside if the three survivors deliver.

participants.name: Hans Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Xander T2, Morikawa T6, Kitayama T34 — Cam Smith and Alex Noren filed for retirement.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele is co-leading at -3. Collin Morikawa shot a 65 and sits sixth. Kurt Kitayama T34. Cam Smith, Alex Noren and Nick Taylor all missed the cut. Two legitimate contenders at the top of the board is a remarkable situation for someone ranked 24th overall. Hans Edstrand is having a quietly stunning US Open.

participants.name: David Rose
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T7, Ryo Hisatsune T11 — Rahm's 78 was a catastrophic second major betrayal.
participants.blurb: Rahm gave you a PGA T2, then showed up at Shinnecock and shot 78. Sam Burns T7 and Ryo Hisatsune T11 are your weekend horses. Chris Gotterup T60. Nicolai Hojgaard and Sepp Straka both missed the cut. The anchor snapped in R2 but there are still two players capable of a big Sunday.

participants.name: Dino Konasewich
participants.blurbShort: McNealy T11, Justin Rose T22, Cameron Young T34 — Shane Lowry and JJ Spaun are done.
participants.blurb: Justin Rose T22, Maverick McNealy T11, Cameron Young T34. Shane Lowry, JJ Spaun and Rasmus Hojgaard all missed the cut. Three players with genuine upside is a solid R3 position. If McNealy keeps playing the way he has, Dino makes a respectable run this weekend.

participants.name: Kit Matkaluk
participants.blurbShort: JT T7, Cameron Young T34 — Patrick Cantlay missed the cut and Marco Penge was never there.
participants.blurb: JT shot 68 and is T7 — your horse is running. Cameron Young T34, Chris Gotterup T60, Jacob Bridgeman T60. Patrick Cantlay missed the cut. Marco Penge not in the field. JT is doing all the work. The question is whether Gotterup or Young can move into the top 20 on the weekend and add some supporting income.

participants.name: David Puddicombe
participants.blurbShort: Jon Rahm CUT. Bryson CUT. Jason Day WD. Patrick Reed CUT. Daniel Berger CUT. Sepp Straka CUT. A historic shutout.
participants.blurb: All six players are either cut, withdrawn, or never in the field. Jon Rahm shot 78. Bryson shot 75. Jason Day withdrew before he teed off. Patrick Reed missed by one. Daniel Berger missed by three. Sepp Straka missed by two. The projected US Open earnings are exactly zero dollars. This has never happened to anyone in this pool across three majors combined. The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale cannot come soon enough.

participants.name: Craig Langdon
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T6, Harman T11, Woodland T11 — a quietly excellent US Open card.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa shot 65 in R2 and sits sixth. Brian Harman is T11. Gary Woodland T11. Ludvig Aberg T22, Russell Henley T46. Daniel Berger missed the cut. Craig Langdon is having the best major of his season at exactly the right time. Four players in solid weekend positions — if Morikawa charges, this could be a game-changing Sunday.

participants.name: Stephen Roberts
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T2, Morikawa T6, McNealy T11, Woodland T11 — four players in the top 11.
participants.blurb: The most underrated card at Shinnecock. Matt Fitzpatrick T2, Collin Morikawa T6, Maverick McNealy T11, Gary Woodland T11, Ben Griffin T34. Cam Smith missed the cut. Five of six made it to the weekend and four are inside the top 11. Stephen Roberts is quietly having a sensational US Open. Watch this space.

participants.name: Nigel Coleclough
participants.blurbShort: JT T7, Ben Griffin T34 — JJ Spaun and Jake Knapp missed the cut. In your own Cup. 31st.
participants.blurb: It is literally your Cup and you are sitting 31st. JT shot 68 and is T7 — genuinely excellent. Ben Griffin T34, Matsuyama T60, Jordan Spieth T46. JJ Spaun missed the cut at +8. Jake Knapp missed the cut. The eponymous host of the Coleclough Cup needs JT to win outright on Sunday to generate any narrative redemption whatsoever.

participants.name: Hugh Cooper Jr.
participants.blurbShort: Xander T2, Fitzpatrick T2, Bhatia T11, Conners T22 — four co-leaders and near-leaders. Best card in the bottom half.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele and Matt Fitzpatrick are co-leading at -3. Akshay Bhatia is T11 at even par. Corey Conners T22. Patrick Reed and Si Woo Kim both missed the cut. Four players in the top 22 going into the weekend — this is the best-positioned card in the bottom half of the overall standings by a considerable distance. A clean Hugh Cooper Jr. Sunday could make the final standings very interesting.

participants.name: Dionne Delesalle
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T6, Justin Rose T22 — Jason Day WD, three others cut.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa shot 65 and is sixth. Justin Rose T22. Jason Day withdrew. Adam Scott, Nicolai Hojgaard and Daniel Berger all missed the cut. Morikawa and Rose are doing the lifting. If Morikawa keeps this form into Sunday, Dionne makes a meaningful jump in the standings despite losing four of six this week.

participants.name: Ted Reid
participants.blurbShort: JT T7, Tommy Fleetwood T22, MacIntyre T60 — Harris English and Patrick Reed both gone.
participants.blurb: JT is T7 and doing his bit. Tommy Fleetwood T22, Robert MacIntyre T60, Jordan Spieth T46. Harris English and Patrick Reed missed the cut. JT is the engine, Fleetwood is the support act, and everyone else is filling seats. Ted Reid needs a JT top-five Sunday to move meaningfully.

participants.name: Rob Snowden
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T6, Cameron Young T34, Gary Woodland T11 — Matt McCarty shot 80 in R2.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa is sixth and carrying this entire roster. Cameron Young T34, Gary Woodland T11. Jake Knapp, Si Woo Kim and Matt McCarty all missed the cut. Matt McCarty shot 80 in R2 — a round that deserves its own footnote in Coleclough Cup history. Morikawa is doing all the work, again, as he has all season.

participants.name: Mark Kaufmann
participants.blurbShort: Xander T2, Conners T22, Harman T11, Tyrrell Hatton T34 — four live horses.
participants.blurb: Xander T2, Brian Harman T11, Tyrrell Hatton T34, Corey Conners T22. Harris English and Robert MacIntyre both missed the cut. Four weekend players including a co-leader is a genuinely solid hand. Mark Kaufmann is in the top 40 overall but this US Open could be the major that changes the narrative.

participants.name: Mark Trotzuk
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T22, Kurt Kitayama T34 — Jason Day WD, Shane Lowry, JJ Spaun all gone.
participants.blurb: Justin Rose is your best at T22. Kurt Kitayama T34. Jason Day withdrew, Shane Lowry missed the cut, JJ Spaun missed the cut, Ryan Gerard missed the cut. Two of six playing the weekend. Rose and Kitayama need to go low on the weekend to generate anything meaningful. The roster was held together with tape going in and is now held together with Rose and a prayer.

participants.name: Jamie Finkbeiner
participants.blurbShort: Xander T2, Bhatia T11, Conners T22, Cameron Young T34 — Sepp Straka and Alex Noren exited quietly.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele co-leading at -3. Akshay Bhatia T11. Corey Conners T22. Cameron Young T34. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren both missed the cut. Four players in the top 35 heading into the weekend — Jamie Finkbeiner has a genuinely competitive US Open card. If Xander wins, this is the major that puts Jamie back in the conversation.

participants.name: Bryson Milley
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T6, Tommy Fleetwood T22, Keegan Bradley T22 — Cam Smith cut, Max Homa absent.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa shot 65 and is sixth. Tommy Fleetwood T22, Keegan Bradley T22. Patrick Cantlay, Cam Smith missed the cut. Max Homa not in the field. Three live players including Morikawa is a better situation than the overall rank suggests. It is what it is — and right now, it's quietly decent.

participants.name: JP Wright
participants.blurbShort: McNealy T11, Tyrrell Hatton T34, Woodland T11 — Bryson and Brooks both missed the cut.
participants.blurb: Bryson DeChambeau and Brooks Koepka both missed the cut — the full LIV power package delivering nothing as promised. Tyrrell Hatton T34, Maverick McNealy T11, Gary Woodland T11, Jordan Spieth T46. The bench players outperformed the headliners by a wide margin. McNealy is your unexpected hero and you should back him accordingly.

participants.name: Stu Louie
participants.blurbShort: Xander T2, Bhatia T11 — Brooks Koepka shot 77 in R2 and left the premises at +10.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele is co-leading the tournament. Akshay Bhatia T11. Jordan Spieth T46. Brooks Koepka shot 77 in R2 and missed the cut at +10 — the loudest possible exit. Min Woo Lee and Nicolai Hojgaard also gone. Xander is the only reason to watch, and what a reason. Two players, big weekend potential.

participants.name: Aaron Watson
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T22, Sungjae Im T34 — Rahm missed the cut, Shane Lowry too, Viktor gone.
participants.blurb: Your Masters was silent. Your PGA was Rahm. Now Rahm has missed the cut at Shinnecock with a 78 and taken half your season with him. Tommy Fleetwood T22, Sungjae Im T34, Nico Echavarria T60. Shane Lowry and Viktor Hovland also gone. Three players in the weekend field but none of them are threatening the leaderboard.

participants.name: Darren Moscovitch
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T7, Aberg T22, Bhatia T11, Conners T22 — four live horses. Quietly your best major.
participants.blurb: Sam Burns T7, Akshay Bhatia T11, Ludvig Aberg T22, Corey Conners T22. Viktor Hovland and Sepp Straka both missed the cut. Four players in the top 25 is a genuinely strong US Open hand. This is quietly Darren's best major of the season — the question is whether the weekend confirms it.

participants.name: Chris Morrison
participants.blurbShort: Theegala T7, Tommy Fleetwood T22, Henley T46 — Patrick Reed, Adam Scott both cut.
participants.blurb: Sahith Theegala is T7 and your surprise star. Tommy Fleetwood T22, Russell Henley T46, Robert MacIntyre T60. Patrick Reed and Adam Scott missed the cut. Sepp Straka gone. Theegala is carrying this card on a weekend where he was supposed to be the afterthought. Chris Morrison is cautiously optimistic for the first time this season.

participants.name: James Patillo
participants.blurbShort: Wyndham Clark leads the tournament at -7 and James Patillo is 45th. The paradox of the season.
participants.blurb: Wyndham Clark is leading the US Open. You have him on your card. You are 45th overall. This is the Coleclough Cup's greatest paradox. Viktor Hovland, Min Woo Lee, Shane Lowry and Daniel Berger all missed the cut. Ludvig Aberg T22 is your only other live horse. If Clark wins, James Patillo makes the biggest single-major move in the pool's history. From 45th to the conversation in 72 holes.

participants.name: Chris MacDougall
participants.blurbShort: Bhatia T11, Cameron Young T34, MacIntyre T60 — Shane Lowry and Jake Knapp both cut.
participants.blurb: Akshay Bhatia T11, Cameron Young T34, Robert MacIntyre T60, Jacob Bridgeman T60. Shane Lowry and Jake Knapp both missed the cut. Four players in the field. Bhatia is your best hope and he's been quietly solid all week. After the worst PGA Championship in the pool, Chris MacDougall is showing signs of life.

participants.name: Kevin Spooner
participants.blurbShort: Brian Harman T11 is your only real horse — Bryson, Reed, Si Woo, Min Woo all gone.
participants.blurb: Brian Harman T11 and Hideki Matsuyama T60 are your weekend players. Bryson DeChambeau, Patrick Reed, Min Woo Lee and Si Woo Kim all missed the cut. Harman is playing well and has a legitimate shot at the top ten on Sunday. Whether that's enough to move Kevin up the standings depends on everyone else having a bad weekend too.

participants.name: Geoff Stollery
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T2, Theegala T7, Bhatia T11, Ryo T11 — five of six made the cut. Remarkable survival rate.
participants.blurb: Matt Fitzpatrick T2, Sahith Theegala T7, Akshay Bhatia T11, Ryo Hisatsune T11, Ludvig Aberg T22. Nicolai Hojgaard missed the cut. Five of six in the weekend field with four inside the top 25 — this is the best roster survival rate in the entire pool. Geoff was posting the lowest Masters total in the Cup; now he's positioned to make a massive US Open statement.

participants.name: Chris Gibbins
participants.blurbShort: Keegan Bradley T22, Jordan Spieth T46 — Bryson CUT, Jason Day WD, Patrick Cantlay CUT.
participants.blurb: Bryson DeChambeau missed the cut. Jason Day withdrew. Patrick Cantlay missed the cut. Keegan Bradley T22, Jordan Spieth T46, Michael Kim T46. Three players in the weekend field. Praying remains the strategy, but at least three prayers are still valid.

participants.name: Fraser Reid
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T22, Keegan Bradley T22 — JJ Spaun, Patrick Cantlay, Max Homa all gone.
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood T22 and Keegan Bradley T22. Harris English, JJ Spaun, Patrick Cantlay and Max Homa all missed the cut. Two horses, two weekends to make something happen. Fleetwood and Bradley would both need career-defining Sundays for Fraser to move up meaningfully. Not impossible. Historically unlikely.

participants.name: Chris Ellison
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T6, Tommy Fleetwood T22, Niemann T46 — Shane Lowry cut, Alex Noren cut.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa shot 65 and is sixth — your sleeper just became your season. Tommy Fleetwood T22, Joaquin Niemann T46. Shane Lowry and Alex Noren both missed the cut. Stephan Jaeger not in the field. Morikawa is the engine and he's firing on all cylinders. A Morikawa Sunday charge could be the best thing to happen to Chris Ellison all year.

participants.name: Tim Kehler
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T2, Ben Griffin T34, Keegan Bradley T22 — JJ Spaun and Ryan Gerard both cut.
participants.blurb: Matt Fitzpatrick T2, Keegan Bradley T22, Ben Griffin T34. JJ Spaun missed the cut at +8. Alex Noren and Ryan Gerard gone. Three live players with a co-leader. Fitzpatrick as a co-leader is the best news this card has had since the Masters. If Fitz wins, Tim Kehler has something to talk about on the 19th hole.

participants.name: Theo Ong
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T2 is carrying this entire team. JJ Spaun, Jake Knapp, Si Woo, Rickie all CUT.
participants.blurb: Matt Fitzpatrick T2. Jacob Bridgeman T60. JJ Spaun missed the cut. Jake Knapp missed the cut. Si Woo Kim missed the cut. Rickie Fowler missed the cut. Fitzpatrick is single-handedly keeping this card alive. If he wins outright, Theo Ong moves up the standings significantly. If he doesn't, the US Open ends on Friday for five of six picks.

participants.name: Russell Ball
participants.blurbShort: Maverick McNealy T11 is your hero — Bryson CUT, two Hojgaards CUT.
participants.blurb: Maverick McNealy T11 is the best thing on this card and nobody saw that coming. Chris Gotterup T60, Jacob Bridgeman T60. Bryson DeChambeau missed the cut. Nicolai and Rasmus Hojgaard — two Hojgaards — both missed the cut. You built a roster out of vibes, surnames, and Maverick McNealy. McNealy is delivering. The rest is decoration.

participants.name: Tom Puddicombe
participants.blurbShort: JT T7, Tommy Fleetwood T22, Sungjae Im T34 — four players alive. Your brother David has zero.
participants.blurb: JT T7, Tommy Fleetwood T22, Sungjae Im T34, Robert MacIntyre T60. Adam Scott and Sepp Straka missed the cut. Four players in the weekend field and JT in the top ten. Meanwhile your brother David has zero players remaining at Shinnecock. Family gatherings will have a new conversation topic. Tom is quietly having his best major since the PGA.

participants.name: Chris Smiley
participants.blurbShort: Akshay Bhatia T11 is your only surviving player. Dead last. The only direction is up.
participants.blurb: Dead last in the entire Coleclough Cup. Akshay Bhatia made the cut at even par and is T11 — your sole survivor. Bryson DeChambeau missed the cut. Brooks Koepka missed the cut at +10. Min Woo Lee missed the cut. Jason Day withdrew. Marco Penge not in the field. One player, 56th place, mathematically able to finish higher. Bhatia needs to shoot 60 on Sunday to generate any meaningful movement.
